channel.title: Fuel for the Sole
channel.description:
Fuel for the Sole is a podcast hosted by Believe in the Run and sports dietitian, Meghann Featherstun. Together they answer your questions about nutrition, hydration and the effects it has on performance. They’ll discuss the latest tech gadgets, nutrition products on the market and bring in the occasional specialist.
Share your questions! Email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured on the show.
Disclaimer: This content is not intended to be a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified healt

item.title: Move Her Mind Summit | Ep 2 | Makenna Myler, Valarie Allman and Taliyah Brooks
item.description: Our final conversation of the Move Her Mind Summit brings together ASICS elite athletes Makenna Myler, Val Allman, and Taliyah Brooks for an inspiring and candid discussion. Each of these remarkable women shares her journey into sport, what fuels her drive to compete, the obstacles she has faced and overcome, and her vision for 2026 and beyond. It was such a fun conversation, and we hope you enjoy listening as much as we enjoyed being part of it.

item.title: 126 | A DQ Blizzard Study, Berberine, Urolithin, Santa Madre RESET gels and MCT Oil
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we break down a “study” making the rounds on Instagram about DQ Blizzards and tackle several supplement questions, including berberine, urolithin, MCT oil, and the Santa Madre RESET gel.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: Move Her Mind Summit | Ep 1 | Taliyah Brooks and Allyson Felix
item.description: To kick off the Move Her Mind Event Summit, we sat down with ASICS athlete Taylia Brooks and Olympian Allyson Felix to explore the different stages and transitions throughout life. From high school competition to the world stage, they shared lessons from their journeys at the highest level—reminding us that every chapter brings its own challenges, growth, and opportunities.

item.title: 125 | Low Carb Research Studies, High Liver Enzymes, Feeling Like the Tin Man and Bloating
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, Tim Noakes has entered the chat. We break down a new research study suggesting that just 10 grams of carbs per hour may be sufficient—and that carb loading might be unnecessary after all. Plus, we tackle listener questions on what could be driving high liver enzymes, what might be sabotaging your recovery, and why bloating shows up mid-run.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠. 

This episode is fueled by ASICS and RNWY!

Head over to⁠ ASICS.com⁠ and sign up for a OneASICS account. It’s completely free and when you sign up you will receive 10% off your first purchase. You also gain access to exclusive colorways on ASICS.com, free standard shipping, special birthday month discounts and more.

Disclaimer: This content is not intended to be a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition.

item.title: 124 | Food Pyramids, Hypernatremia, Iron Supplementation, and Muscle Building vs Weight Loss
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we briefly touch on the new food pyramid before diving into listener questions—including the risks of overdoing sodium and hypernatremia, recommendations for iron supplementation, and whether the off-season is better spent focusing on muscle building or weight loss.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: 123 | 2026 Nutrition Trends, Ferritin and Altitude, Nomio and Gut Training
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we’re diving into the predicted nutrition trends for 2026 — from the rise of high-fiber and high-protein foods to a renewed focus on gut health and minimally processed eating. We also tackle listener questions on ferritin and altitude, the buzzy new supplement Nomio, and whether you really need to train with the exact same gel you plan to use on race day.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: 122 | The Running Event Recap, Generation UCAN Labels, Primal Queen and More
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we break down our biggest takeaways from The Running Event — from exciting new brands to the trends shaping the future of endurance fueling. We also dive into some eye-opening information we uncovered about Generation UCAN (and yeah… it was pretty wild). To wrap it up, we answer listener questions on cutting back fiber before race day and take a hard look at a new supplement called Primal Queen, which may be the ultimate case study in what not to take — ever.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: 121 | Fiber Maxxing, Nitrates and Hot Dogs, Mid-Race Hydration Fails
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we dive into the new trend of “fiber maxxing” and why it’s probably not for us. We also tackle listener questions on fueling and race intensity, whether downing a bunch of hot dogs can replace a nitrate supplement, and if it’s possible to “catch up” in a marathon after missing a few water stops.


Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: 120 | Nicotine Trends, Creatine and Caffeine, Ketone-IQ Bottles and More
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we break down Thomas’ latest algorithm—one that’s somehow convinced he (and everyone else) should be taking nicotine. We also dig into the trending supplement creatine, answering your top questions, including the best time to take it and the difference between creatine HCL and monohydrate. Plus, we cover the Tokyo Marathon’s hydration rules and what runners need to know before race day.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: 119 | New York City Marathon, Hyponatremia, Hitting the Wall, and Fueling with Sponge Cake
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we recap the highlights from the New York City Marathon and share the latest updates on the LA100 Group. We also tackle listener questions on several topics, including hyponatremia, hitting the wall, the difference between ferritin and iron, and why sponge cake might just be the ultimate pre-run fuel for your big workouts and races.

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠.

item.title: 118 | Lead in Protein Powders, Carb Loading with Gels, Maintaining Ferritin Levels and More
item.description:
This week on Fuel for the Sole, we’re celebrating Meghann’s third time taking (and passing!) her CSSD exam — and breaking down what that means and why it matters. We’re also diving back into the hot topic of lead in protein powders after a new study stirred up quite a bit of conversation. Plus, we answer your listener questions on: carb loading with gels, practical ways to add more fruits and veggies into your diet and maintaining healthy ferritin levels. 

Want to be featured on the show? Email us (written or an audio file!) at⁠ fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com⁠. 

This episode is fueled by ASICS and RNWY!

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 12 | Jess Wedel and Skye Latimer
item.description: The 12th stop of the Move Her Mind Event Series with @asics brought us to Oklahoma! We got to chat with Jess Wedel, mountain guide and cancer survivor and Skye Latimer, lifelong dancer and joy-bringer. Jess shared her experience of summiting Mount Everest, and how she leads teams through some of the world's most challenging terrain, while Skye shared her passion for dance at age 4 lead to dancing on sold-out stages and choreographing music videos. This conversation was so fun and inspiring and we hope you enjoy it.

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 13 | Deena Kastor
item.description:
Stop 13 (yes, we’re swapping stops 12 and 13!) of the Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS took us all the way to Tokyo! 

We had the incredible opportunity to sit down with Deena Kastor - legendary marathoner and true ambassador of the sport - to talk about her journey from professional runner to passionate advocate. Deena shares her perspective on the barriers women have faced in running, the biggest shifts she’s witnessed over the years, and, of course, her take on the women’s marathon world record being broken - nearly twenty years after she set her own.

This episode wraps up with something truly special: the next chapter of the Move Her Mind Event Series. 

We’re thrilled to announce a new partnership with ASICS that will allow us to bring 100 women to the LA Marathon! Selected participants will receive personalized nutrition coaching and training plans from The Megs, plus a race bib and a full VIP race-day experience.

Want to join us in LA? Apply through THIS LINK —we can’t wait to see you there!

item.title: 116 | Hot Marathon Season, Influencer Advice, Proper Carb Calculating and Salty Gels
item.description:

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 11 | Allie Caminiti, Art Therapist and Run Coach
item.description:
The 11th stop of the Move Her Mind Event Series with @asics brought us to Decatur, GA!  There, we had the chance to sit down with Allie Caminiti—art therapist, EMDR therapist, and run coach—to explore her journey into mental endurance training and ultramarathoning.

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 7 | Asia Rawls
item.description:
The 7th stop of the Move Her Mind Event Series landed us in Grand Rapids! We sat down with Asia Rawls, ASICS Ambassador and member of WeRUN313. A Division 1 NCAA qualifier and record holder from Eastern Michigan University, Asia has always championed the profound impact of running on both body and mind. She furthered her education at the University of Detroit Mercy, earning an MBA and an M.S. in Computer Information Systems, while also serving as a coach.

Currently, Asia is a community leader and the owner and founder of Machine Athletics, LLC, where she offers expertise in sprints, strength, speed, and long distance. We hear all about how Asia got her start in running and how it’s evolved through all the stages of life. 

We’ve got several more stops, so join us at a city near you and bonus points if you bring a friend who needs your support & encouragement to start a movement habit. And if you’re not able to attend in person, we’ll be sharing all of these conversations on Fuel for the Sole.

For more details: https://www.asics.com/us/en-us/move-her-mind/

item.title: Disordered Eating Series | Ep 3 | REDs and Running
item.description:
In the third and final episode of our Disordered Eating Series, we dive into Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (REDs) and its impact on runners. We break down what REDs is, the key signs and symptoms to look out for, and how under-fueling can affect hormones and overall health.

We also discuss the recovery process—how to rebuild from a chronic energy deficit, the role of hunger cues in healing, and the often underestimated energy demands of running, daily life, and breastfeeding combined.

If you're ready to start your recovery journey, reach out to Lyndsie to see if her services are available in your state. You can reach out to Lyndsie at Lyndsie@fncohio.com or 614-285-6677. You can also find Lyndsie on social media or visit her website for more information: 

Instagram: Fulfilled Nutrition Counseling - Instagram

Facebook: Fulfilled Nutrition Counseling - Facebook

Website: www.fulfillednutritioncounseling.com


This episode is sponsored by the Eating Recovery Center. Struggling with an Eating Disorder? Get Help Now - 866.489.5497 or schedule a free assessment at www.EatingRecoveryCenter.com. Looking for support? Check out our complimentary peer-led eating disorder and mental health support groups at https://www.eatingrecoverycenter.com/support-groups

item.description:
The sixth stop of the Move Her Mind event series with ASICS took place in the vibrant New York City! In this episode, we sit down with ASICS athlete Emma Grace Hurley and Lauren Nemeroff, co-founder of Flow State Creative, for an inspiring conversation about the power and resilience of female athletes.

We explore how Lauren captures the strength and spirit of the women she photographs, the unique pressures Emma Grace faces as a professional runner, and the ways she stays grounded through training and competition. Together, we dive into themes of showing up for yourself, breaking through barriers, and rediscovering the joy in movement.

item.title: Fuel for the Sole | Disordered Eating Series | Ep 2 | Helping with Disordered Eating Thoughts as a Runner
item.description:
In the second episode of our Disordered Eating Series, we explore the lingering disordered thoughts that can persist after eating disorder treatment and throughout the recovery journey. We discuss practical strategies for reducing negative food thoughts and old behaviors, and why it's crucial to ensure these thoughts don’t shape how we nourish our children or talk about food in our homes.

We also take a look at UCAN’s GLP-1 marketing claims and the broader messages circulating on social media—highlighting how to recognize harmful narratives and protect ourselves from being triggered during recovery.

If you're ready to start your recovery journey, reach out to Lyndsie to see if her services are available in your state. You can reach out to Lyndsie at Lyndsie@fncohio.com or 614-285-6677. You can also find Lyndsie on social media or visit her website for more information:

Facebook: Fulfilled Nutrition Counseling - Facebook

Website: www.fulfillednutritioncounseling.com

item.title: Disordered Eating Series | Ep 1: Recovery from Eating Disorders as a Runner
item.description:
In the first episode of our Disordered Eating Series, we tackle the complex and deeply personal topic of recovering from eating disorders while maintaining a relationship with running. We answer listener questions and bring in expert insight from Lyndsie N. Romes, a registered dietitian with nearly a decade of experience working in the field of eating disorders. Lyndsie helps us unpack what recovery can look like for runners, how to recognize disordered patterns, and how to rebuild a healthy connection with both food and movement. Whether you're on your own recovery journey or supporting someone else, this conversation is a powerful starting point.

If you're ready to start your recovery journey, reach out to Lyndsie to see if her services are available in your state. You can reach out to Lyndsie at Lyndsie@fncohio.com or 614-285-6677. You can also find Lyndsie on social media or visit her website for more information: 

Instagram: Fulfilled Nutrition Counseling - Instagram

Facebook: Fulfilled Nutrition Counseling - Facebook

Website: www.fulfillednutritioncounseling.com

We answer listener questions and bring in expert insight from Lyndsie N. Romes, a registered dietitian with nearly a decade of experience working in the field of eating disorders. Lyndsie helps us unpack what recovery can look like for runners, how to recognize disordered patterns, and how to rebuild a healthy connection with both food and movement. item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 5 | Britta Gauthier Aho – Motion PT
item.description:
The fifth stop of the Move Her Mind event series with Asics was held in Minneapolis, MN!  In this episode we chat with Britta Gauthier Aho, a pelvic health PT who works with runners through all stages of life, including pregnancy, perimenopause and menopause. We discuss how to modify your training and nutrition during these phases of life and what you can expect from your pelvic health PT. 

We’ve got several more stops, so join us at a city near you and bonus points if you bring a friend who needs your support & encouragement to start a movement habit. And if you’re not able to attend in person, we’ll be sharing all of these conversations on Fuel for the Sole.

For more details: https://www.asics.com/us/en-us/move-her-mind/

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 4 | Abby Lokits and Lolita Sneed-Bryant
item.description:
The fourth stop of the Move Her Mind event series with Asics was held in Nashville, TN. In this episode we speak with Lolita Sneed-Bryant, an RN, Certified Run Coach and Certified Health Coach and Abby Lokits, Executive Director of Free to Move, a non-profit advocating for Women’s Safety. Free to Move was started in memory of her late sister-in-law, Alyssa Lokits, who was shot and killed on a popular running trail in Nashville on October 14, 2024. We discuss tactics and resources for coming back from trauma, women’s safety and delineating between anxiety and fear. 

In this discussion, Lolita mentions a coronary calcium scan, also called a heart scan, which is a non-invasive CT scan used to assess coronary artery disease (CAD) risk. It helps doctors determine the amount of calcified plaque (a buildup of calcium deposits) in the heart's arteries. This information, along with other risk factors, helps estimate the likelihood of experiencing a heart attack. 

You can learn more about Free to Move and find resources here: https://www.wearefreetomove.org/ 

We’ve got several more stops, so join us at a city near you and bonus points if you bring a friend who needs your support & encouragement to start a movement habit. And if you’re not able to attend in person, we’ll be sharing all of these conversations on Fuel for the Sole.

For more details: https://www.asics.com/us/en-us/move-her-mind/

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 3 | Makenna Myler and Megan Connolly Morant
item.description:
The third stop of the Move Her Mind event series with Asics was held in Ridgefield, CT. In this episode we chat with Asics-sponsored professional runner, Makenna Myler, who lives and trains in Utah. She placed 7th at the 2024 US Olympic Marathon Trials where she clocked a time of 2:26:14, just 10-months after giving birth to her son. We’re also joined by Megan Connolly Morant –a television and multi-media personality, best known for her work with WWE. She is also an avid marathon runner, a former Division I cross-country runner at Northwestern University and a podcast host for Getting Chicked. We discuss forming habits and routines around movement, motherhood and a whole lot more.

We’ve got several more stops, so join us at a city near you and bonus points if you bring a friend who needs your support & encouragement to start a movement habit. And if you’re not able to attend in person, we’ll be sharing all of these conversations right here on Fuel for the Sole.

For more details: https://www.asics.com/us/en-us/move-her-mind/

item.title: Move Her Mind Event Series with ASICS | Ep 1 | Dr. Dee Dlugonski and Tenia Fisher
item.description: We kicked off the Move Her Mind event series with ASICS in DC at the Cherry Blossom 10-Miler. This year, the Megs will be traveling around the country, hosting group runs and panels and bringing in experts to chat on all sorts of topics - keeping the conversations meaningful and impactful with the goal of getting more women moving. To kick-off the series, we headed down to DC to host a conversation about the results of ASICS’ global study on the gender exercise gap with study co-chair Dr. Dee Dlugonski and ASICS Sound Mind, Sound Body Ambassador, Tenia Fisher.We’ve got several more stops, so join us at a city near you and bonus points if you bring a friend who needs your support & encouragement to start a movement habit. And if you’re not able to attend in person, we’ll be sharing all of these conversations right here on Fuel for the Sole. For more details: https://www.asics.com/us/en-us/move-her-mind/

item.title: 62 | A new study on reactive hypoglycemia, carb loading and more listener questions
item.description:
This week we dive into a new study that discusses the association between pre-exercise food ingestion timing and reactive hypoglycemia. The study examined a group of nearly 7,000 users who logged a total of 48,799 pre-exercise food events with CGM data. We chat about: 

  • What exactly is reactive or rebound hypoglycemia 
  • How to navigate reactive hypoglycemia
  • Carb loading tactics 
  • Why you might be feeling hungry all the time  
  • A whole lot more! 

Want to be featured on the show? Email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com.

item.title: 49 | Our 3 Biggest Nutrition Mistakes
item.description:

✔ Why it’s important to fuel your long runs

✔ Why leg cramps might be related to hydration

✔ How Ferritin changes affect performance and why it’s important to get levels checked regularly

✔ How under-fueling during the taper affects race day

✔ A whole lot more!

item.title: 48 | CBD & THC for runners
item.description:
This week we sit down with Jesse Stanley, founder of Charlotte’s Web, a CBD company. Their whole-plant CBD extracts come in full-spectrum and broad-spectrum (THC-free) options, including the world's only broad-spectrum CBD certified NSF for Sport. In this episode, we talk to Jesse about:

  • The difference between CBD and THC
  • How CBD affects endurance athletes
  • What to look for (and avoid) when selecting your CBD products
  • Where CBD is headed in the future
  • A whole lot more!

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 41 | Adjusting for Race Day Weather & Recovery
item.description:
We’ve made it through the fall marathon season, and many of the races were warmer than we expected! So, what can we do mid-race when the weather doesn’t align with our goals? We dive into several scenarios and tactics, along with some post-race recovery. We discuss:

✔️ What to do in the days leading up to a marathon when the weather will be 60+ degrees 

✔️ The causes behind mid-race nausea and GI distress

✔️ Why you may be developing muscle cramps late in the race

✔️ Reflecting on your race and adjusting for the future

✔️ A whole lot more!

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 40 | Treating deficiencies and improving training with supplements
item.description:
Today we tackle a bunch of listener questions, all about supplements! We review how athletes are and should be using them and the risks associated with this unregulated industry. We cover:

✔️ When to take supplements: treating deficiencies and improving training

✔️ The benefits of Omega-3s for endurance athletes

✔️ The importance of timing and Collagen supplementation

✔️ If we really need green powders/supplements

✔️ A whole lot more!

item.title: 39 | Sleep for performance with Dr. Shelby Harris
item.description:
Today’s episode of Fuel for the Sole, we have special guest Dr. Shelby Harris on the show! Dr. Shelby Harris is a licensed clinical psychologist who specializes in behavioral sleep medicine (BSM), working to improve the sleep of everyone from babies through older adults using evidence-based methods. In this episode, we discuss:

✔️ How sleep impacts athletes and how many hours we actually need

✔️ Traveling for races and changing time zones

✔️ Sleep hygiene and bedtime routines you should be implementing

✔️ Melatonin, magnesium and other sleep aids

✔️ A whole lot more!

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: Fall Marathon Race Recap: Breaking down a 6+ Minute Marathon PR [Bonus Episode]
item.description:
BONUS EPISODE! We’re calling this one a “Fall Marathon Race Recap” but really, we just discuss every detail of Meghann Featherstun's 6+ MINUTE PR at the Chicago Marathon. We dive into all of it, including:

✔️ How Meghann carb loaded leading up to race day

✔️ The changes she made throughout this training cycle

✔️ A play by play of the race, including when she took in gels and hydration 

✔️ What Meghann attributes to her successful race day

✔️ And a whole lot more!

This is a fun one, guys!

item.title: 38 | Dealing with Contradicting Nutrition Advice
item.description:
In today’s episode of Fuel for the Sole, we discuss contradictory nutrition advice. We debunk and discuss several topics including:

✔️ Whether or not women can actually carb load

✔️ How many carbs per hour we can metabolize during a race

✔️ If race fuel is dependent upon your size and gender

✔️ If you should use real food or gels when racing

✔️ A whole lot more!

item.title: 27 | Fueling for two-a-days, increasing ferritin levels and race day hydration
item.description:
In today’s episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer more listener questions! First, we talk about fueling two-a-days, followed by a discussion on ferritin and iron levels and a few more hydration and electrolyte questions. We cover:

✔️  Best fueling strategies when working out twice a day

✔️  Understanding iron and ferritin levels

✔️  How to increase ferritin levels through food and supplementation

✔️  The best ways to ingest sodium and electrolytes while racing

✔️  If you should only drink water with your gels

✔️  A whole lot more!

item.title: 26 | Nutrition periodization for enhanced training adaptations
item.description:
In today’s episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer two listener questions. First, we talk about nutrition periodization -- a planned, purposeful, strategic nutrition approach to enhance training adaptations, followed by a discussion on cooking oils and the different types of fat. We cover:

✔️  The goal of Nutrition Periodization

✔️  The three main phases of training

✔️  How to adjust nutrition based on training load

✔️  The difference between saturated, unsaturated and trans fats

✔️  The different types of cooking oil and their uses

✔️  A whole lot more!

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 25 | Race day carb loading, nutrition mishaps and solutions
item.description:
In today’s episode of Fuel for the Sole, we talk about preparing for race day! We answer follow up questions from our initial carb loading discussion and chat through race day nutrition mishaps and solutions. We cover:

✔️  Fuel utilization during running and racing

✔️  The optimal time to carb load

✔️  How to calculate your personal carb intake requirements

✔️  Weight gain during carb loading

✔️  Mid-race tactics for nausea and GI distress

✔️  A whole lot more!

Download Meghann’s Carb Loading Guide: https://www.featherstonenutrition.com/carb-loading/

RSVP for the Boston Bagel Run: https://bit.ly/BostonBagelRun

item.description:

✔️  What the data from food sensitivity tests actually means

✔️  The probable causes of your bloating and GI distress

item.title: 22 | The latest nutrition wearables for runners
item.description:
In this Episode of Fuel for the Sole, we discuss some of the latest nutrition wearables, including Nutrisense and Lumen. We cover:

✔️  Why everyone is talking about metabolic flexibility

✔️  What a CGM (continuous glucose monitor) is and what it’s used for

✔️  Who can benefit from tracking this information

✔️  The data and nutrition technology we expect to see in the future

✔️  A whole lot more!

item.title: 21 | Protein for endurance athletes
item.description:
In this Episode of Fuel for the Sole, we discuss all things protein. Sports dietician, Meghann Featherstun, gives us a solid overview on the different types of protein, how much we (runners) need on a daily basis, and the importance of protein timing. We also discuss:

✔️  What to look for when choosing a protein powder

✔️  When you should use a collagen supplement

✔️  If plant based proteins are OK for endurance athletes 

✔️  Some less common, ideal protein sources

✔️  A whole lot more!

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

This show is sponsored by @InsideTracker! For a limited time, InsideTracker is offering our listeners 20% off their entire store. Visit InsideTracker.com/fuel.

item.title: 20 | Weight loss and running
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Cory’s question about weight loss. We’ve covered this topic in Episode 4, but we keep receiving questions about weight loss while running, so we’re tackling it again. This time we talk about:

✔️  The first steps to take when prioritizing weight loss

✔️  Why energy balance is so important

✔️  Myths about fat, willpower and metabolism

✔️  A whole lot more!

item.title: 19 | Sugar and performance nutrition
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Jamie’s question about sugar. It’s pretty common for runners to worry about their sugar consumption, so we’re here to set the record straight. We discuss separating daily vs. performance nutrition and dive into:

✔️  The difference between added and natural sugars

✔️  Should we (runners) follow AHA and FDA guidelines for sugar intake

✔️  Myths about high fructose corn syrup and alcohol

✔️  Whether you should opt for real or artificial sugar

✔️  A whole lot more!

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

This show is sponsored by @InsideTracker! For a limited time, InsideTracker is offering our listeners 25% off their entire store. Visit InsideTracker.com/fuel.

item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we bring back Mary Johnson from Life Run Perform to chat all about the Breaking 3 Project. This one goes a little off topic (don’t they all?), but we think you’re going to love it. We discuss:

✔️  Coming back to running after pregnancy

✔️  How the Breaking 3 Project came to be and what it’s all about

item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Elliott’s question about caffeine. We discuss:

✔️  How much caffeine is too much caffeine

✔️  Should you cut back on caffeine before a big race

✔️  Does caffeine enhance performance

item.title: 16 | All things calories
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Austin and Elsie’s questions about all things calories. We discuss:

✔️  How to calculate your daily calorie needs

✔️  Why your fitness tracker is probably not accurate

✔️  Whether or not you should fluctuate calories based on daily activities

✔️  The difference between counting calories and counting macros

​​✔️  The importance of calorie timing and a discussion on intermittent fasting

item.title: 15 | Race Specific Nutrition
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Mack’s question about fueling for a half marathon vs. a marathon. We discuss carb loading for the different distances and the nutrition and hydration strategies for each. We also dive into:

✔️  The best time to fuel during a half marathon

✔️  Why you shouldn't take fuel for certain distances / effort levels

✔️  Hydration strategies from the 5K to the marathon

✔️  And some candid discussion around body composition concerns and performance

item.title: 14 | Who to trust when it comes to sports nutrition
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Elisa’s question about who to trust. We dive into Meghann’s credentials and what makes her a professional along with the red flags to look for when ingesting advice on Instagram. We also chat about:

✔️  Why food elimination is not the best strategy  (unless we have an allergy)

✔️  Why we’re all talking about ‘burning fat for fuel’

✔️  Who we need to listen to as performance based runners

✔️  Some bonus content about how our hydration needs change through the winter

item.title: 13 | Alcohol and training
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Toms’ question about drinking alcohol during training. We discuss the effects of alcohol on performance and whether or not you should eliminate alcohol entirely before a big race. We also chat about ‘junk food’ and the best times to incorporate these foods into your training.

item.title: 12 | Nutrition for recovery
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Chris’ question about post run recovery. We discuss whether or not you should be using recovery drinks, the appropriate carb and protein breakdown, and the recovery window you really need to nail. We also chat about alcohol, what supplements can help reduce inflammation and the role of hydration.

item.title: 11 | Race day fueling for optimal performance
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Ally’s question about how much fuel to take on race day. We chat about whether you should use mile markers or timing for nutrition reminders, how to strategically use caffeine on the course, where to store all your fuel, if you need to adjust your strategy based on weather, and a whole lot more.

item.title: 10 | Taper Week and Carb Loading
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Jaclyn’s question about taper week. We discuss what your nutrition should look like the days leading up to race day and how the body stores fuel. We chat about how to effectively carb load, what race distances require carb loading and why practicing race day fueling ahead of time is so important. Trying to figure out your carb loading plan? Download your free guide: https://mailchi.mp/1fbc32d0dbf2/carb-load-guide

item.title: 09 | Fueling for 'peak week'
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Nick G’s question about using nutrition to aid recovery. We dive into the ‘peak week’ of training and discuss the roles that sleep, hydration and nutrition have in your recovery. We talk about anti-inflammatory foods, supplements, and how to adjust your macros as you increase training volume and intensity.

item.title: 08 | Optimizing nutrition and hydration for your menstrual cycle
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Kara Lombardo’s question about the menstrual cycle and how we can use nutrition to help alleviate some of the negative side effects. We discuss the three phases of your cycle, what to expect from your training during each of these phases, and how to adjust your nutrition and hydration to align. We also discuss birth control, supplements and why racing on your period might actually be the best time.

BONUS: (36:37) We answer Philip Kallenberg’s question about what type of carbs we should be eating and when.

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 07 | The common, "little" mistakes runners make
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Nick Alexander’s question about the little mistakes runners make. We discuss why calorie counting and using apps like MyFitnessPal can lead to bad habits, the importance of balancing nutrition throughout the day, and how we can all normalize taking nutrition out on the run. We also chat about the misconception of gels and GI issues, myths about hydration, and if you actually need to take supplements.

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 06 | How carbs affect performance and how to scale them based on activity
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Sarah Hanson’s question about carbs! We dive into the science behind carbs, why they are the preferred fuel source for endurance athletes and how many grams per day you need, regardless of activity level. Meghann also gives us her take on intuitive eating, signs you may be under fueling, and some of the amazing non-performance related benefits of eating sufficient carbs.

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 05 | Building muscle through running, lifting and nutrition
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Kelly Maxey's question on how to build muscle through nutrition, running and strength training. To help us answer this question, we bring in Lift, Run, Perform Founder and Coach, Mary Johnson. We discuss the importance of nutrient timing, how to effectively fuel for a run + strength workout, and why endurance athletes are able to see vast improvement to body composition and performance by incorporating strength training with proper nutrition. 

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 04 | Balancing weight loss while training
item.description:
In this episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Grant Den Herder's question about weight loss while training, a very popular topic among runners. We dive into why it’s important to prioritize our goals and how to find our optimal body weight for performance. We also discuss the importance of nutrition timing and properly fueling our runs. Lastly, we discuss the mental side of nutrition, including disordered thoughts about food and body image. 

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 03 | Selecting the best long run fuel
item.description:
In the third episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Tamanna Singh's question about Generation UCAN, the effects of slow releasing carbs vs. fast releasing carbs and what "burning fat for fuel" really means. We also talk about how to choose the best fuel source and some of the science behind Maurten. Lastly, we follow up on Thomas' blood test results after his marathon and the effects of the vaccine. 

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: 02 | Fueling through Injury for a Faster Recovery
item.description:
In the second episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Carrie Johnson's question about fueling through injury. We discuss the importance of protein, helpful supplements for healing faster and why nutrition may be the reason you're getting injured in the first place. We also dive into the two main types of injuries for runners: bone (stress fracture, reaction) and soft tissue (tendon, ligament, muscle) and the best approaches for each. Lastly, we talk about Thomas' latest marathon and recovery.

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

This show is sponsored by InsideTracker! For a limited time, InsideTracker is offering our listeners 25% off their entire store. Visit InsideTracker.com/fuel.

item.title: 01 | Hydrating for Performance
item.description:
In the first episode of Fuel for the Sole, we answer Caryn Just's question about hydrating properly in the Summer. We discuss the importance of knowing your daily hydration needs, sweat and sodium rates, and how to acclimate to the Summer heat and humidity faster. We also dive into some common misconceptions about hydrating, the effects of alcohol and why you shouldn't take nutrition advice from non-sports dietitians on Instagram. 

Want to be featured on the show? Share your questions via the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button) or email us at fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com to be featured.

item.title: Fuel for the Sole Intro
item.description: Fuel for the Sole is a podcast hosted by Believe in the Run and sports dietitian, Meghann Featherstun. Together they answer your questions about nutrition, hydration and the effects it has on performance. They’ll discuss the latest tech gadgets, nutrition products on the market and bring in the occasional specialist. Send in your questions on the Anchor App (just click the 'message' button on this page: anchor.fm/fuelforthesole) or email them to fuelforthesolepodcast@gmail.com!
